name: Shapes Around Us — Circles, Squares, Triangles
whatIsIt: Everything you see has a shape. A CIRCLE is perfectly round with no corners. A SQUARE has 4 equal sides and 4 corners. A TRIANGLE has 3 sides and 3 corners. A RECTANGLE has 4 sides too, but two are long and two are short. We name a shape by counting its SIDES and CORNERS.
whyNeeded: Shapes are everywhere — a roti is a circle, a window is a rectangle, a slice of watermelon is a triangle. Naming shapes helps us describe the world, build with blocks, and later learn about area, symmetry and drawing.
realLifeUses: A chapati and the full moon are circles
A window and a door are rectangles
A slice of cake or samosa looks like a triangle
A carrom board and many tiles are squares

vocabulary.meaning: A straight edge of a shape.
The point where two sides meet.
A perfectly round shape with no sides and no corners.

story.title: Anya's Shape Walk
story.text: Anya walks around her house with Papa, hunting for shapes. 'The clock is round like a circle,' she says. 'The window has four sides — two long, two short — it is a rectangle!' Then she finds a slice of watermelon. 'This one has three sides and three corners,' says Papa. 'What shape is it?' Anya counts and smiles.
story.extractedProblem: A watermelon slice has 3 sides and 3 corners. What shape is it?

visual.caption: Count sides and corners: circle (0), triangle (3), square (4), rectangle (4).

teachingGallery.title: Name it by counting sides & corners
teachingGallery.note: No corners = circle. 3 = triangle. 4 equal = square. 4 with long & short = rectangle.

standardMethod.summary: To name a flat shape, count its corners (or sides).
steps: Look for corners. No corners at all → it is a circle.
4 corners → square (if all sides look equal) or rectangle (if two are long, two short).

workedExamples.problem: A road sign has 3 straight sides and 3 corners. Name the shape.
steps: 3 corners and 3 sides is a triangle.

workedExamples.problem: A biscuit is perfectly round with no corners. Name the shape.
steps: Look for corners — there are none.
No corners means it is a circle.

commonMistakes.fix: A square has 4 EQUAL sides. If two sides are long and two are short, it is a rectangle.

commonMistakes.mistake: Counting sides or corners wrongly, so a triangle gets called a square.
commonMistakes.fix: Touch each corner once as you count. A triangle has exactly 3; a square/rectangle has 4.

hintLadder: Corners are pointy — a circle has none.

easy.q: How many corners does a triangle have?

medium.q: A window has two long sides and two short sides. What shape is it?
hintLadder: Two are long, two are short.

medium.q: How many sides does a square have?
hintLadder: A square has equal edges all around.

challenge.q: A carrom board has 4 equal sides. Ravi calls it a rectangle. What is it really?

options.label: Square — all four sides are equal

challenge.answer: Square — all four sides are equal
hintLadder: Rectangles have long and short sides.

challenge.q: A shape has 3 corners. How many sides does it have?
hintLadder: Corners and sides match in these shapes.

questions.q: Which shape has 3 sides and 3 corners?
How many corners does a rectangle have?
hintLadder: Count the corners of a window.

questions.q: The full moon is shaped like a…
A shape with 4 sides where two are long and two are short is a…

teachBackPrompt: Take your grown-up on a 'shape walk' around one room. Find a circle, a square, a rectangle and a triangle, and tell them how you know each one by counting sides and corners.

revisionCard.summary: Name a shape by its corners: 0 corners = circle, 3 = triangle, 4 equal = square, 4 (long & short) = rectangle. Sides and corners match in number.

rememberIt.hook: Count the sides to name the shape
rememberIt.unpack: 3 sides = triangle, 4 = square/rectangle, 5 = pentagon, 6 = hexagon. A circle has no straight sides - it is a curve.

studentQuestions.q: How do I tell a square from a rectangle?
studentQuestions.a: Both have 4 sides and 4 square corners. A square's sides are ALL equal; a rectangle has two long and two short.

studentQuestions.q: How many sides does a circle have?
studentQuestions.a: None straight - it is one smooth curved line all the way round.
